character_setup.title: Your story
character_setup.body:
What is your name? It doesn't have to be your real name!

Pick a look — you'll grow and change as the years go by.

start_age_8.title: Age 8
start_age_8.body: You are eight years old. School is fine, but weekends feel like the whole world. Your backpack is heavy with homework you keep forgetting. What pulls you in most right now?

choices.label: Hide in the library with a stack of books
Race friends until the streetlights buzz on
Figure out games on the old family computer
Beg for a phone and get lost in feeds and quick videos

year_09.title: Age 9
year_09.body: You're nine. Long division shows up and refuses to make sense. Where do you put the extra energy?

choices.label: Extra worksheets and flashcards until it clicks
Bike loops around the block until your legs buzz
Trade silly jokes at recess and forget half the homework

year_10.title: Age 10
year_10.body: Ten feels like a big round number. Someone says "double digits" like it's a promotion. What do you lean into?

choices.label: A science fair volcano and glitter everywhere
Quiet reading and a secret notebook of stories
Join pickup games until you're muddy and grinning
Blow allowance on candy and playground trades
Beg for the sparkly shoes everyone has

year_11.title: Age 11
year_11.body: Eleven is the year adults start asking about "middle school" like it's a weather forecast. You practice being older in small ways.

choices.label: Organize a closet fundraiser for the class trip
Pretend you don't care and watch cartoons instead
Start learning an instrument; your fingers hurt

age_12_school.title: Age 12
age_12_school.body: You turn twelve. Teachers talk about "paths" like you are already on a train track. You pick something to care about hard enough that lunch conversations change.

choices.label: Join the robotics club and lose sleep over a motor
Say yes to theater crew — heavy boxes, big laughs
Try a team sport even though you're nervous

year_13.title: Age 13
year_13.body: Thirteen arrives with weird voice cracks or awkward silences. Group chats splinter. You pick a lane for the year.

choices.label: Study streaks and honor roll energy
Skate park, scraped knees, new playlists
Part-time help at a family friend's shop

year_14.title: Age 14
year_14.body: Fourteen: high school looms on the calendar. You can taste both freedom and pressure. What's your move?

choices.label: Map out classes like a general planning a campaign
Say yes to every invite until you're exhausted
Train for something physical — miles or mats

teen_15.title: Age 15
teen_15.body: Phones are everywhere. Everyone's life looks edited and shiny. You still have chores, a part-time allowance, and opinions that don't fit in a caption.

choices.label: Mute a few feeds and pick one real hobby instead
Post constantly — maybe the likes will feel like friends
Install every trending app and scroll until your eyes burn
Start a tiny online project (stickers, tips, silly videos)
Default to fast food when you're late, stressed, or bored
Try vaping with friends — cool now, lungs later
Party weekends — say yes to shots every time

year_16.title: Age 16
year_16.body: Sixteen: driver's ed pamphlets, first real paycheck fantasies, someone you like who might not know your name.

choices.label: Stack shifts and save for a beater car
Volunteer weekends; sleep in on Mondays
Fancy dinner and flowers for someone you like — wallet screams
Tunnel vision on grades for scholarship rumors
After-school Spanish — slow mouth, laughs, bad accents
French club — films with subtitles and ego bruises
Saturday Mandarin — tones that fight back

year_17.title: Age 17
year_17.body: Seventeen is college tours, trade school flyers, or neither. Everyone has advice; your head hums.

choices.label: Tour campuses and stress-eat free mints
Shadow someone at work and imagine that life
Road trip weekend with friends — cheap motels
Prom night: limo split, outfit, photos — teenage theater at full volume

year_18.title: Age 18
year_18.body: Eighteen lands like a door unlocking. Voting, contracts, and tiny print. You're still under your parents' roof, but the lease on childhood is almost up. The feed still thinks you're a kid. In some countries, military or national service is the shadow behind the birthday cake — optional here, but real if you choose it (discipline and strength; strain; a tiny chance you don't come back whole, or at all).

choices.label: Sign up for military or intensive national service — harder body, heavier head, rare lethal or disabling risk
Open a real bank account and read every fee
Throw a chaotic birthday and worry about the cleanup
Ghost the group chat for a week and walk outside
Skip PE and long walks — stay on the couch with screens

social_media_beat.title: The feed
social_media_beat.body: One night you read a thread about careers you've never heard of. The algorithm also serves you ads for colleges, bootcamps, and "six-figure skills." Your savings jar rattles when you shake it.

choices.label: Research seriously: compare college, work, and trade school
Close the tab and pretend adulthood is far away

year_19.title: Age 19
year_19.body: Nineteen: you're not at your parents' house on paper anymore — dorm, first lease, or a friend of a friend. Rent and deposits hit your assets the day this year begins. Cash still leaks in dumb places.

choices.label: Meal prep Sundays; boring but steady
Concert tickets on credit you swear you'll pay
Pick up gig apps when cash runs thin

year_20.title: Age 20
year_20.body: Twenty feels like a checkpoint. Half your friends post milestones; half disappear. Another year of rent or room charges ticks by.

choices.label: Start a real budget spreadsheet
Say yes to a messy trip you can't quite afford
Double down on certifications or night classes
Conversational language studio — get awkward on purpose
You're trying to be a creator: post with a trending audio — it dies in an hour
Cheap clip somehow hits the For You page — dopamine, dread, and a tiny creator fund

year_21.title: Age 21
year_21.body: Twenty-one: IDs checked, advice from relatives, small wins and small panics. Housing still isn't free.

choices.label: Negotiate a raise with shaking hands
Move cities for a fresh start
Stay put and build routines that stick
Creator life: refresh analytics until 3am — views flat, self-esteem lower
One idea pops: not life-changing money, but DMs from tiny brands and a high you can't trust

invest_young_adult.title: Odd jobs and apps
invest_young_adult.body: Your account finally looks like something. Everyone has an opinion: index funds, "safe" yields, parlays, or coins with a dog on them.

choices.label: Buy a broad stock index and try not to panic when it dips
Park money in bonds and treasury-style savings — boring on purpose
Chase quick wins with sports betting apps and live odds
YOLO into crypto threads, wallets, and 3 a.m. charts

year_22.title: Age 22
year_22.body: Twenty-two: first real "career" bruises or weird wins. You learn which meetings could've been emails. Rent still shows up every year.

choices.label: Network awkwardly at industry meetups
Protect evenings like they're sacred
Take a certification exam on no sleep

year_23.title: Age 23
year_23.body: Twenty-three: comparisons itch. Someone buys a car; someone moves abroad; you refill the coffee maker. One more year of paying for a roof.

choices.label: Therapy copay feels worth it
Side project that might never pay
Overtime streak for a bonus that might not come
Sponsored post misfires — comments turn feral, you pretend you don't care
Wholesome trend rides the wave — mostly likes, a little hope, still not a salary

year_24.title: Age 24
year_24.body: Twenty-four: you can almost see the shape of your twenties. Big choices stop feeling hypothetical.

choices.label: Finally move in with chosen roommates or solo
Pay down debt aggressively; rice and beans season
Adopt a pet on impulse

where_you_base.title: Where you're based
where_you_base.body: Twenty-four redraws the map. Plenty of people stay rooted where they grew up. Pro hoops and similar leagues often pull talent toward the United States—drafts, college pipelines, and shiny contracts. Founders sometimes chase the same geography for investors and brutal hours. A few go full nomad: visas, short lets, carry-on life, no address that sticks long.

choices.label: Stay based where I grew up — same country as family roots
Move to the United States for draft, NCAA pipeline, or pro league shots (NBA and beyond)
Relocate to the United States for startup density and capital (long visas, burned savings)
Base in a European hub for a fresh market (London–Berlin axis; heavy rent shock)
Go nomad — no fixed country, rolling time zones and short lets

young_adult_fork.title: Almost grown
young_adult_fork.body: You are in your mid-twenties. People you trust still disagree about what "smart" looks like. Assets, rent, equity, and student-sized habits all compete. College costs a lot up front, but it can sharpen your mind for years after. Your parents watch how you've been doing — grades, mood, friends, vices — and slide you a lump sum when you commit to a path (bigger if they approve, stingier if you look messy).

choices.label: Apply to college — big loans, roommates, brutal reading lists
Skip degree for now and take a full-time job
Gap year: work part-time and travel cheap

vacation_styles.title: Where to disappear
vacation_styles.body: You clear the calendar. Trips cost real cash — and they pay you back in mood, friends, and sometimes your lungs. Pick the kind of escape you can almost afford.

choices.label: Caribbean cruise — islands, buffet gravity, midnight decks
Fly to Europe — one week, capital highlights, brutal jet lag
Euro backpacking month — hostels, trains, too many cities
US national parks road trip — trails, storms, dumb luck
Resort week — warm water, cold drinks, one pool you'll remember
Japan — cities, temples, trains that run on shame and pride
Staycation — couch, local hikes, guilt about not going farther (cheap)

vacation_return_router.title: Back through customs
vacation_return_router.body: You return with a camera roll of almost-good photos and a body that remembers walking. Normal life resumes — until the next itch to leave.

choices.label: Step back into your chapter
Step back into your chapter
Step back into your chapter

midlife_after_college.title: Later on
midlife_after_college.body: Your twenties slide into your thirties. Classes are behind you, but loans and rent still argue every month. Leadership floats a high-visibility project — the kind that eats nights and weekends. Or you could finally block two weeks, drain savings a little, and go somewhere that isn't a screen.

choices.label: Take a real vacation — beaches, trains, or sleeping in somewhere new
Say yes to the extra project and keep pushing the career ladder

midlife_after_work.title: Later on
midlife_after_work.body: Years of shifts, deadlines, and small raises add up. You're not scraping by anymore, but you're running on fumes. Your manager hints at a promotion track that means more travel and more email. Your savings could buy silence — a trip long enough to remember what a weekday without alarms feels like.

choices.label: Book time off and go on vacation instead of chasing the next title
Double down: take the stretch role and keep stacking hours

midlife_after_gap.title: Later on
midlife_after_gap.body: You never quite snapped back to a single lane — gigs, seasons, cheap flights when you could. The cushion in your account is real but nervous. An offer lands: a longer contract, steady pay, less sky. Or you could blow a chunk of the stash on one long unplugged trip before the next chapter.

choices.label: Choose the long trip — rest now before the next grind
Take the steady contract and postpone the dream vacation

life_37_college.title: The degree still echoes
life_37_college.body: You're in your late thirties. Paper still opens doors; interest rates still whisper at night. Peers swap stories about layoffs and side projects. You feel the first real fork between roots and runway.

choices.label: Invest in roots — steadier housing, a partnership, fewer cross-country pivots
Keep chasing the title — bigger job, brighter résumé, thinner margins

life_37_work.title: Momentum and mileage
life_37_work.body: Late thirties. You know how shifts feel in your knees and your inbox. Some friends bought houses; some roommates became legends. You're carrying more competence than credit — and wondering if that's enough.

choices.label: Trade up carefully — better pay, night classes, small cushion
Stack overtime and side gigs while you still can

life_37_gap.title: Scrapbook and spreadsheets
life_37_gap.body: Late thirties, and your path still refuses a single headline. Stability flickers — sometimes a lease, sometimes a friend's couch between gigs. You're rich in stories and nervous about the next decade.

choices.label: Anchor somewhere — sign the lease, set down a toolbox, learn the neighbors
Stay light on your feet — another season of maybe

life_40_converge.title: Forty and the fire alarm tests itself
life_40_converge.body: Forty lands like a bookmark. Some people show you baby photos; others show spreadsheets. You notice who's tired, who's numb, and who still laughs loud at bad jokes. You choose what this decade owes you.

choices.label: Grow the family — sleepless years, loud love, long obligations
Protect the dyad or solo path — fewer dependents, clearer calendars
Step into elder care for a parent — expensive, heavy, meaningful

life_50_prime.title: Fifty — legacy stops being abstract
life_50_prime.body: Half a century. Colleagues joke about reading glasses and mortality; you notice they aren't really joking. The net worth line on your screen finally means something different. What kind of weight do you want to carry forward?

choices.label: Mentor publicly — open office hours, messy honesty, small ripples
Endow a local fund — serious check, serious paperwork
Put your name on a scholarship — the big visible gift
Quietly shore up the nest — retirement knobs, boring funds, fewer headlines

life_58_flow.title: Fifty-eight — momentum meets maintenance
life_58_flow.body: Joint pain shows up in weather forecasts. Promotions start to look like traps. Someone you love asks what you believe about money when the lights go out. You still have moves.

choices.label: Fund a free clinic month — medicine as the gift
Slow the career on purpose — trade title for mornings
Medical weight program — prescription meds, coaching, labs (very expensive)
Push one more big project — stack assets while the body agrees

life_68_reflect.title: Sixty-eight — the world speeds up without you
life_68_reflect.body: Retirement is a word people use about you. You notice memory glitches that aren't funny yet. Friends disappear into hospitals and headlines. The will isn't hypothetical anymore — it's a file someone could open.

choices.label: Repair a family rift while there's still time
Rest and routines — walks, doctors, small stubborn joys
One last gamble — swing at a venture, a cause, a spotlight

life_76_elder.title: Seventy-six — kindness is logistics
life_76_elder.body: The days narrow. Helping someone dress isn't romantic until it is. Money becomes less about flex and more about who eats when you're gone.

choices.label: Choose gentleness — smaller goals, clearer goodbyes
Fight every inch — treatments, trials, denials

life_84_will.title: Eighty-four — the ledger and the heart
life_84_will.body: Lawyers use calm voices. Your people use brave ones. What happens to the house, the scraps of dignity, the causes you cared about after headline season ended?

choices.label: Family first — lean the estate toward kin, small gifts elsewhere
Balanced will — heirs fed, charities named, arguments minimized
Charitable focus — most of what remains turns outward

life_epilogue.title: The ledger rests
life_epilogue.body: The story pauses here — not because life is small, but because every telling needs an ending. What you planted shows up in other people's budgets, grudges, scholarships, and jokes. Rest.

death_natural.title: Lights quiet down
death_natural.body: Health finally asked for something you couldn't negotiate. The numbers stop, the pain stops, the calendar stops. What you built and gave keeps moving without you — sometimes fairly, sometimes not. That part isn't yours to steer anymore.

prologue_north_star.title: Before it starts
prologue_north_star.body: Before the years start clicking by, one question sits like a compass in your chest. If you could fast-forward to the future, what do you want to be when you grow up?

choices.label: Engineer / builder — solve problems, ship things
Doctor / healer — help people when it matters
Artist / creator — make something that lasts
Social media creator — clips, trends, likes that own your mood
Entrepreneur — take risks, build your own thing
Athlete — train hard, compete, win
Teacher / mentor — make others better
Traveler — collect places instead of stuff
No idea yet — I'll figure it out

north_star_check_11.title: North star check-in
north_star_check_11.body: You catch yourself daydreaming about the future again. If you had to answer right now: what are you projected to be when you grow up?

choices.label: Still: Engineer / builder
Still: Doctor / healer
Still: Artist / creator
Still: Entrepreneur
Still: Athlete
Still: Teacher / mentor
Still: Traveler
Still: Social media creator
Still: no fixed plan yet
Switch: Entrepreneur
Switch: Athlete
Switch: Teacher / mentor
Switch: Traveler
Switch: Social media creator — feed, metrics, and overnight fantasies
Honestly: still undecided

north_star_check_16.title: North star check-in
north_star_check_16.body: You catch yourself daydreaming about the future again. If you had to answer right now: what are you projected to be when you grow up?

choices.label: Still: Engineer / builder
Still: Doctor / healer
Still: Artist / creator
Still: Entrepreneur
Still: Athlete
Still: Teacher / mentor
Still: Traveler
Still: Social media creator
Still: no fixed plan yet
Switch: Entrepreneur
Switch: Athlete
Switch: Teacher / mentor
Switch: Traveler
Switch: Social media creator — chase the algorithm instead of a title
Honestly: still undecided

north_star_check_21.title: North star check-in
north_star_check_21.body: You catch yourself daydreaming about the future again. If you had to answer right now: what are you projected to be when you grow up?

choices.label: Still: Engineer / builder
Still: Doctor / healer
Still: Artist / creator
Still: Entrepreneur
Still: Athlete
Still: Teacher / mentor
Still: Traveler
Still: Social media creator
Still: no fixed plan yet
Switch: Entrepreneur
Switch: Athlete
Switch: Teacher / mentor
Switch: Traveler
Switch: Social media creator — posters, reels, and brand deals in daydreams
Honestly: still undecided

housing_decision.title: Housing at 24
housing_decision.body: You are staring at the hardest balance sheet of young adulthood: rent checks leak cash every month, but a mortgage ties you down and eats your assets up front. If you buy a place and rent it out, you still need somewhere to sleep — factor in your own cheap room or crash pad. Going unhoused skips the lease but chews through health and happiness.

choices.label: Stay unhoused — couch surf, car nights, shelters when it breaks you (no lease, brutal toll)
Keep renting — first/last + moving costs; no deed, predictable cash drain
Buy a starter condo ~$285k · 5% down + closing (mortgage, tight budget)
Buy a townhouse ~$395k · 10% down (bigger mortgage, more space)
Buy a single-family ~$495k · 15% down (classic mortgage path)
Buy a fixer ~$210k · 10% down + rehab fund (cheap purchase, heavy work)
Duplex ~$540k · 20% down, live one unit and rent the other (mortgage + tenant income)
Buy a small rental ~$360k · 15% down, but YOU rent a cheap room elsewhere (two housing bills)

setHome.label: Rental owned · you rent a room elsewhere

choices.label: Rent a room in a shared house and sublet when you travel (side income, still paying rent)
